title: Idempotency: track migrated rows in Postgres brain.migration_log
userStory: As an operator, I want migrated Notion items tracked so that re-running the migrator after edits updates only what changed.
acceptanceCriteria: compose/docker-compose.yml ships a postgres service on the brainnet (compose-managed, named volume for persistence, healthcheck on pg_isready). No host port mapping on the VPS; the local overlay (docker-compose.local.yml) binds 5432 to 127.0.0.1 for laptop runs
.env.example documents POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DB, and a BRAIN_DATABASE_URL connection string
Migration creates table brain.migration_log with PRIMARY KEY (notion_page_id) and columns: target_id (the database or page id passed on the CLI), notion_last_edited, graphiti_episode_id, migrated_at
On re-run: unseen page_id -> migrate + insert; seen page_id with newer notion_last_edited -> re-migrate (relying on Graphiti bi-temporal invalidation) + update row; otherwise skip
Skipped rows are logged with reason
Verify: run twice with no Notion changes -> second run reports 0 migrated, N skipped; edit one row in Notion, re-run -> exactly that row re-migrated
